pr.title: Fix: Use container names (bt-postgres, bt-redis) for Docker backend env vars
pr.body:
## Summary

Updates `docker-compose.yml` to use Docker container names (`bt-postgres`, `bt-redis`) instead of service names (`postgres`, `redis`) for the backend's `DB_URL` and `REDIS_URL` environment variables. These are hardcoded in docker-compose.yml so they always resolve correctly inside the Docker network, regardless of what `.env` contains.

Also adds clarifying comments to `.env.example` explaining that `DB_URL` and `REDIS_URL` in `.env` are for local dev only — Docker Compose overrides them with container-name-based URLs.

**Changes:**
- `docker-compose.yml`: `DB_URL` → `jdbc:postgresql://bt-postgres:5432/billiontech`, `REDIS_URL` → `redis://bt-redis:6379`
- `.env.example`: Added notes explaining local vs Docker networking for DB and Redis URLs

## Review & Testing Checklist for Human

- [ ] Run `docker compose down -v && docker compose up --build` and verify the backend connects to Postgres and Redis (check logs for successful Flyway migrations and no connection errors)
- [ ] Run backend locally with `./mvnw spring-boot:run` using `.env` values to confirm local dev still works with `localhost:5433` / `localhost:6380`

### Notes

Both service names (`postgres`, `redis`) and container names (`bt-postgres`, `bt-redis`) are resolvable on the Docker Compose default network. This change aligns the URLs with the explicit `container_name` values for consistency and clarity.

Link to Devin session: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/28f22dd6a7d44cc8b0cd820ac982a0cf
Requested by: @ranganvaradan
pr.author: devin-ai-integration[bot]
